items.title: Adani Flagship Seeks $1 Billion Via Share Placement, Terms Show
items.description: Adani Enterprises Ltd. is seeking to raise as much as 100 billion rupees ($1 billion) through a sale of shares to institutional investors as the ports-to-power conglomerate led by billionaire Gautam A

items.title: BlackRock Partner Securitize Rides Wall Street Tokenization Wave
items.description: Carlos Domingo spent years trying to persuade investors that stocks, bonds and private funds would one day live on blockchains — an idea that stayed largely confined to crypto’s fringes.

items.title: US Adds Fewer Jobs Than Expected in June, Unemployment Rate Falls
items.description: US hiring slowed sharply in June as nonfarm payrolls increased 57,000 last month after downward revisions to the prior two months, according to Bureau of Labor Statistics data out Thursday. Michael Mc

items.title: De la Espriella Says Colombia Will Seek to Refinance Debt
items.description: Colombian President-elect Abelardo de la Espriella said his incoming administration will seek to refinance the nation’s debt to extend maturities and lower borrowing costs.

items.title: Treasuries Gain as Weak Jobs Report Dims Fed Rate-Hike Bets
items.description: Treasuries rallied after a weaker-than-expected jobs report prompted traders to scale back expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates in the coming months.

items.title: Two Blue Owl Private Credit Funds Impose Caps After Redemption Requests
items.description: For the second straight quarter, two Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit funds were hit with the industry’s largest redemption requests, forcing the manager to again cap withdrawals.

items.title: US Hiring Slows Sharply, Curbing Recent Job-Market Momentum
items.description: US hiring slowed sharply in June even as the unemployment rate fell, curbing some of the budding momentum in job growth this year.

items.title: US Stocks Advance as Jobs Data Dims Prospect of Fed Rate Hike
items.description: US stocks rose before the bell on Thursday as cooler-than-expected employment data drove traders to dial-back bets that the Federal Reserve could start raising interest rates as soon as this month.

items.title: CoreWeave Junk Bonds Slide Further as Investors Question AI Boom
items.description: Bonds issued last month by AI infrastructure firm CoreWeave Inc. fell for a second day running after reports that Meta Platforms Inc. is developing plans for a competing cloud business hit its shares

items.title: Tech Rally Chasers Make Calls Priciest Since 2007 Next to S&P
items.description: Normally, a three-month, 30% rally in the Nasdaq 100 Index would tempt investors to take the money and run. But right now, they’re betting the advance is just getting started.

items.title: Banks Get Bids on $49 Billion Paramount Debt Well Before Sale
items.description: Banks have lined up enough investor demand to cover most of the roughly $49 billion debt package backing Paramount Skydance Corp.’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. — long before launchin

items.title: Genneia Files for the First Argentine IPO in the US Since 2019
items.description: Power producer Genneia SA filed for an initial public offering in New York, trying to become the first Argentine company to IPO in the US in at least seven years.

items.title: Saudi Oil Flows Hit 90% of Pre-War Rate as Ships Exit Hormuz
items.description: Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports have surged close to their pre-war levels as the kingdom starts getting its tankers through the Strait of Hormuz again, one of the most emphatic indications yet that P

items.title: US Premarket Movers for July 2, 2026
items.description: S&P 500 Index futures were little changed as of 7:53 a.m. New York time as investors rotate out of the high-flying momentum stocks that powered last quarter’s rally.

items.title: Springbok Emblems May Get Legal Shield Before All Blacks Visit
items.description: South Africa proposed protecting the commercial rights to the Springboks’ upcoming rugby tour against New Zealand’s All Blacks, a move aimed at safeguarding sponsorship revenue.

items.title: Leveraged ETFs Are Just the Tip of the Iceberg
items.description: Record leverage is sweeping through the financial system, exposing hedge funds, banks, retail traders and money market funds to potential mounting losses.

items.title: Bad News for LNG Buyers as Qatar’s Export Revival Falters
items.description: While oil is flowing again through Hormuz, fewer gas tankers are making the still-risky crossing.
